title: Phase separation enhances probability of receptor signalling and drug targeting
research_objectives: Analyze how liquid-liquid phase separation affects receptor tyrosine kinase signaling
Explain the probabilistic nature of signal transduction in cells
Demonstrate how LLPS improves signaling fidelity and specificity
Connect phase separation to drug targeting strategies
Propose LLPS as fundamental regulatory mechanism in signal transduction

research_scope.focus_area: Receptor signaling and phase separation biology

signal_transduction_challenge.problem: Inherently low probability of RTK triggering specific cellular outcome
signal_transduction_challenge.biological_consequence: How do cells achieve specific, reliable responses?

phase_separation_solution.effect: Weights probabilistic signaling toward required outcome
phase_separation_solution.fidelity: Ensures discrete, mutually exclusive cellular responses

compartmentalization_benefits.effector_concentration: High local concentration of signaling effectors
compartmentalization_benefits.specificity_improvement: Ensures committed pathway vs background noise

spatial_organization.location: Plasma membrane and subm embrane regions

signaling_game_perspective.probabilistic_framework: Signaling success requires multiple simultaneous events
signaling_game_perspective.numbers_game: Sufficient receptor and effector density critical
signaling_game_perspective.llps_advantage: Creates high local concentration for productive signaling

drug_targeting_implications.traditional_approach: Inhibit or activate specific signaling proteins
drug_targeting_implications.strategy: Exploit LLPS for improved therapeutic selectivity
